options.text: compares how different papers treat the same one or two stories
lists every article in one newspaper
reviews only the sports pages

exercises.explanation: Thématique = one story, many treatments. Du jour = many front pages, one morning.

exercises.prompt: Your revue uses « dit » three times in a row. The verdict:

options.text: it's a shopping list — vary the reporting verbs
perfect economy of style
« dit » is forbidden in journalism

exercises.explanation: Écrit, souligne, s'interroge, ironise, titre — the loom needs more than one thread.

exercises.prompt: How a paper announces something on its front page.
exercises.template: « Douze jours pour rien », {{b1}} le quotidien en première page.
exercises.explanation: Titrer — to headline. Papers neither sing nor whisper; they headline.

exercises.prompt: A paper published once a week.
exercises.template: L'{{b1}} prend le recul que le quotidien n'a pas.
exercises.explanation: Un hebdomadaire — weekly; the daily sprints, the weekly thinks.

exercises.prompt: The classic way to OPEN a revue de presse:

options.text: une citation percutante — a punchy quote that intrigues
the weather forecast
an alphabetical list of the papers consulted

exercises.explanation: Intrigue first, inform second — the listener must want the next sentence.

exercises.prompt: What each paper does with its chosen angle (two words: mettre en…).
exercises.template: Chaque journal met en {{b1}} un angle différent.
exercises.explanation: Mettre en avant — to put forward, to highlight.

exercises.prompt: Match each press word to its English meaning.
Put the words in order: "Each paper puts forward a different angle."
Listen to this revue de presse fragment. What is the journalist doing?
exercises.transcript: « Un succès diplomatique », écrit le quotidien national ; « douze jours pour rien », ironise son rival — même sommet, deux planètes.

options.text: Comparing two papers' treatments of the same event — the thematic move
Reading one article aloud in full
Giving his own opinion of the summit

exercises.explanation: Two sources, two reporting verbs (écrit / ironise), one event: the revue thématique in one sentence.

exercises.prompt: Read this account of a morning revue de presse and answer both questions.
exercises.passageFr: Sept heures du matin. La chroniqueuse ouvre sur une citation percutante : « Nous avons vendu le ciel », s'indigne l'éditorialiste d'un grand quotidien à propos des données personnelles. Puis elle tisse : l'hebdomadaire économique, lui, met en avant les chiffres du secteur ; la presse régionale s'inquiète pour ses lecteurs ; un journal étranger ironise sur « le pays des droits de l'homme connecté ». Transition élégante, clôture en clin d'œil — elle rend l'antenne en ramenant l'auditeur au ciel vendu de la première phrase.
exercises.passageEn: Seven a.m. The columnist opens with a punchy quote: 'We have sold the sky,' fumes the editorialist of a major daily about personal data. Then she weaves: the financial weekly puts forward the sector's figures; the regional press worries for its readers; a foreign paper quips about 'the land of the connected rights of man.' Elegant transition, closing wink — she hands back the mic by returning the listener to the sold sky of the first sentence.

questions.prompt: How does the columnist treat her four sources?

options.text: She weaves them by their angles — indignation, figures, worry, irony
She reads each article in full, in order of length
She corrects each paper's mistakes
It loops back to the punchy quote that opened the revue
It announces tomorrow's weather
It lists the papers alphabetically

exercises.prompt: Why must the revue name each source's identity (daily/weekly, regional/national)?

options.text: A regional daily and a financial weekly cannot read the same event the same way
It is required by copyright law
To prove the journalist owns many subscriptions

exercises.explanation: The source's nature is part of the analysis — who speaks shapes what is said.

exercises.prompt: Listen, then choose what it means.
exercises.transcript: Même actualité, deux traitements : l'écart est l'information.

options.text: Same news, two treatments: the gap is the story.
'A diplomatic success,' writes one; 'twelve days for nothing,' quips the other.
A press review with three 'says' is a shopping list.

exercises.prompt: What does « une revue de presse » mean?

options.text: a press review
to group together
the handling

exercises.explanation: une revue de presse = a press review.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word (from this unit's vocabulary).
exercises.template: Quotidien ou mensuel : la {{b1}} change le regard.
exercises.explanation: « Quotidien ou mensuel : la périodicité change le regard. » — publication frequency.

exercises.prompt: Match each word to its English meaning.
Speaking practice — listen, then say the sentence aloud and record yourself:
exercises.targetFr: Même actualité, deux traitements : l'écart est l'information.
exercises.targetEn: Same news, two treatments: the gap is the story.

exercises.prompt: Speaking practice — say this sentence aloud and record yourself:
exercises.targetFr: Une revue de presse avec trois « dit » est une liste de courses.
exercises.targetEn: A press review with three 'says' is a shopping list.
